title: The $500 Barbershop Play
objective: Fill weekday chairs and book new regulars by getting a trusted host to send local listeners in for a first-visit deal.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spots on two local hip-hop/culture shows
One live mention with a promo code the host uses himself
Two Story mentions with swipe-up to booking link

whyItWorks: Barbershops live and die on trust and routine, and a host who gets his own cut on-mic is the perfect referral. Loyal local podcast audiences already care about grooming and neighborhood spots, and mid-tier shows are cheap enough to run two of them on a $500 budget.

title: The $650 Soul Food Plate Play
objective: Drive weekend dinner traffic and Sunday plate pre-orders by putting the menu in front of hungry local listeners with a host who actually eats there.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spots describing signature plates on two culture shows
Live mid-show read timed for Friday drop before the weekend
One sponsored blurb with weekend hours and pre-order link

whyItWorks: Nothing sells a plate like a host describing the smell of the mac and cheese between segments. Culture podcasts pull loyal local audiences who treat food recommendations as gospel, and a Friday live read lands right when weekend meal plans get made. Mid-tier show pricing lets a small kitchen run real frequency without a big budget.

title: The $550 Hair Salon Play
objective: Book out stylists' open slots and attract new color and protective-style clients through host recommendations and social proof.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spots on lifestyle and culture shows
Three Story mentions featuring before/after transformation clips

whyItWorks: Hair is a high-trust, referral-driven purchase, and a host vouching for a salon carries the weight of a friend's recommendation. Loyal culture and lifestyle audiences care deeply about self-presentation, and pairing the audio read with transformation clips on Instagram closes the loop from hearing to seeing to booking.

title: The $450 Nail Salon Play
objective: Grow midweek appointments and introduce a new set of seasonal nail designs to local listeners looking for a go-to spot.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spot on a lifestyle/culture show
Two Story mentions with a carousel of recent nail sets
One creator clip showing a design time-lapse with the salon tagged

whyItWorks: Nail art is visual and social, so pairing a trusted host read with short-form clips shows the craft while borrowing the host's credibility. Local lifestyle audiences book beauty services on impulse and loyalty, and a single mid-tier show plus social keeps the whole play inside a $450 budget.

title: The $700 Personal Trainer Play
objective: Generate free-consultation and trial-session leads for a local trainer by tapping into audiences already primed for discipline and self-improvement.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spots on motivation and sports shows with a trackable landing page
Live read where the host shares a personal fitness goal and the offer
Two Story mentions with a lead form link for a free assessment

whyItWorks: Fitness is sold on belief and accountability, and motivation-minded podcast audiences are already leaning into self-improvement when the offer hits. A host framing the trainer around his own goals turns an ad into a testimonial, and a trackable landing page makes every lead measurable on a starter budget.

title: The $800 Tax Season Play
objective: Book tax-prep appointments and consultations during filing season by reaching listeners who want a trusted preparer who understands their situation.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spots across business and culture shows with a booking link
One sponsored blurb with an early-filer discount and deadline reminder
Two Story mentions with quick tax tips and a consult link

whyItWorks: Choosing who handles your money is a trust decision, and a host vouching for a preparer removes the fear of the unknown. Business and culture audiences include gig workers and small-business owners who need real help by the deadline, and running three mid-tier shows regionally builds the frequency that turns filing pressure into booked appointments.

title: The $600 Tattoo Shop Play
objective: Book consultations and fill an artist's calendar by showcasing portfolio work to culture-forward listeners who value custom art.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spots on hip-hop and culture shows
Two Story mentions featuring healed portfolio pieces
One creator clip of a session time-lapse with the shop tagged

whyItWorks: Tattoos are permanent, so people choose an artist on reputation and portfolio, exactly what a host read plus visual proof delivers. Hip-hop and culture audiences prize self-expression and custom art, and pairing audio credibility with time-lapse clips shows skill while keeping the play affordable for a single shop.

title: The $400 Mobile Detailing Play
objective: Book weekend detailing appointments by reaching local car-proud listeners with a convenient come-to-you service and a first-detail offer.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spot on a local culture or sports show with a booking link
Live read where the host books a detail on-air as a demo
Two Story mentions with before/after clips and a text-to-book number

whyItWorks: A clean ride is a point of pride, and a host getting his own car detailed on-air is instant proof the service delivers. Local audiences love convenience, so a come-to-you offer paired with dramatic before/after clips converts, and one mid-tier show plus social keeps the play lean at $400.

title: The $650 Boutique Drop Play
objective: Drive traffic and sales for a new seasonal collection drop by getting culture-forward listeners excited about a limited local release.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spots teasing the drop on hip-hop and lifestyle shows
Three Story mentions with lookbook shots and a drop-day countdown
One creator try-on clip styling the new pieces with the shop tagged

whyItWorks: Fashion drops thrive on hype and taste-makers, and a trusted host teasing a limited release creates the fear of missing out that fills a store on drop day. Streetwear and culture audiences follow style cues closely, and layering podcast buzz with try-on clips turns a small boutique's launch into an event.

title: The $500 Food Truck Play
objective: Draw crowds to the truck's weekly stops and pop-up events by announcing the schedule and menu to hungry local listeners.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read 60s spot naming this week's location and signature item
Live read timed the morning of a stop with the exact address
Three Story mentions with the weekly location map and menu photos

whyItWorks: A food truck's biggest challenge is telling people where it is right now, and a same-day live read solves that better than any billboard. Local culture audiences chase good food and events, and a host hyping the menu the morning of a stop turns casual listeners into a lunch-rush line, all within a $500 budget.

title: The $1,500 Independent Artist Play
objective: Drive first-week streams and pre-saves for a new single by putting it in front of engaged rap fans through trusted hosts. Convert listeners into Spotify follows and Instagram profile visits.

mediaMix.detail: 60-second host-read spots on two hip-hop podcasts with the hook played under the read
Host reacts to the single live and shares the pre-save link in the episode
Two Reels from host accounts using the hook as audio with a swipe-up to stream

whyItWorks: When a host who fans already trust plays your hook and says your name, it lands as a co-sign, not an ad. Loyal rap podcast audiences are exactly the crowd that pre-saves and adds tracks to personal playlists, and a strong hook clipped into Reels can outrun the campaign on its own.

title: The $1,200 Club Night Play
objective: Sell tickets and fill the room for a recurring club night by targeting local listeners who plan their weekends. Build a repeat crowd that comes back monthly.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read spot on a local culture podcast plugging the date, lineup, and ticket link
Three story takeovers counting down to the night with the flyer and RSVP link
Host shouts out the party as their weekend pick with a promo code

whyItWorks: Nightlife runs on word of mouth, and a host naming your party as their pick carries the same weight as a friend's recommendation. Local podcast crowds skew toward the people already going out, and a countdown of stories keeps the date top of mind through the week when tickets actually move.

title: The $800 Open Mic Play
objective: Put butts in seats for a weekly comedy night and grow a regular audience. Position the show as the go-to affordable night out.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read spot on a comedy podcast pitching the show as a cheap laugh-filled night out
Two Reels of past-show crowd clips with the recurring date and location

whyItWorks: Comedy podcast listeners already love live stand-up, so a host recommending your room is a warm handoff to the exact audience you want. Crowd-laughing clips prove the vibe better than any flyer, and shares from people tagging their friends do the heavy lifting for a low-ticket night out.

title: The $2,000 First Drop Play
objective: Sell through a limited first drop and build a mailing list of early supporters. Establish the brand as one to watch in the local scene.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read spots on two culture podcasts telling the founder's story and drop date
Three Reels of hosts wearing the pieces with a link to the drop
Two TikToks unboxing and styling the first pieces with the drop countdown

whyItWorks: A first drop lives or dies on credibility, and a host wearing your pieces on camera lends instant proof that the brand is real. Culture audiences trust taste more than ads, and a styling clip that catches can turn a limited run into a sellout well beyond your follower count.

title: The $900 Creative Booking Play
objective: Book new photo and video clients by reaching creators and small business owners who need content. Fill the calendar with paid sessions.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read spot on a local business or culture podcast offering a first-session discount
Two Reels showing a before-and-after reel and a booking link in bio
Featured blurb in a local creator newsletter with portfolio link and promo code

whyItWorks: People hire creatives whose eye they already like, so a host vouching for your work sends leads who are half-sold before they DM. Podcast and newsletter audiences are full of business owners and artists who constantly need shoots, and one strong portfolio clip circulating can book out a month.

title: The $700 Dance Class Play
objective: Fill weekly class slots and sign up new students on intro packages. Build a loyal roster that renews month to month.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read spot on a culture or wellness podcast promoting a free first class
Two Reels of class choreography with a sign-up link and intro offer

whyItWorks: Trying a new class takes a nudge, and a trusted host saying the first one is free lowers the barrier for people already curious. Culture audiences connect with hip-hop dance as both fun and fitness, and choreography clips are endlessly shareable, pulling in friends who want to come together.

title: The $1,000 Podcast Launch Play
objective: Win first subscribers and downloads for a brand-new show by cross-promoting to established podcast audiences. Build a base of loyal weekly listeners.

mediaMix.detail: Cross-promo host-read swaps on two established culture podcasts driving to the new feed
Two Reels of the best trailer moment with a follow-and-subscribe call to action
One TikTok of a sharp episode clip formatted for the fyp

whyItWorks: Podcast listeners take recommendations most seriously from other podcast hosts, making cross-promo the single most efficient way to find your first real subscribers. Established culture audiences are primed to add another show to their rotation, and a genuinely funny or sharp clip can pull in listeners who never heard the host at all.

title: The $1,300 Grooming Brand Play
objective: Launch a grooming or beauty supply line and drive first online orders. Get product into the hands of barbers and stylists who become repeat buyers.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read spot on a culture podcast built around the host's own grooming routine and a promo code
Three Reels of barbers and stylists using the product on real clients
One TikTok demo of the product with a before-and-after result

whyItWorks: Grooming is personal, so a host folding your product into their real routine reads as a genuine recommendation rather than a pitch. Culture audiences trust barbers and stylists as authorities, and a satisfying before-and-after demo is exactly the kind of clip people save and send, turning one post into steady orders.

title: The $600 Block Party Play
objective: Draw a big turnout for a free community day and connect neighbors with local vendors and sponsors. Build goodwill and a mailing list for next year.

mediaMix.detail: Host-read spot on a local culture podcast framing the day as a can't-miss family event
Two story posts with the flyer, schedule, and vendor lineup

whyItWorks: A community event thrives on local trust, and a host who knows the neighborhood inviting people out makes the day feel like a shared tradition. Local culture audiences care deeply about what happens in their area, and a warm host read spreads through group chats and reshares far faster than a paid flyer would.

title: The $1,100 Book Launch Play
objective: Drive first-week book sales and reviews for a self-published title. Turn early readers into word-of-mouth advocates who leave ratings.

mediaMix.detail: Author interview segment plus a host-read plug with the purchase link and launch-week discount
Two Reels of the host reading a hooking passage with a link to buy
Featured author spotlight in a culture newsletter with an excerpt and buy button

whyItWorks: Readers buy books they hear talked about with real enthusiasm, and a host interview lets your story breathe in a way an ad never could. Culture and self-improvement audiences are loyal buyers who leave reviews, and a well-read passage clipped to Reels can travel far past launch week and keep sales rolling.

title: The $3,000 National Drop Play
objective: Drive coast-to-coast awareness and first-week sales for a limited streetwear drop by embedding the release inside culture podcasts your buyers already trust.

mediaMix.detail: 60-sec mid-roll host reads across 4 mid-tier hip-hop and culture shows timed to drop week
3 host Reels styling the collection with swipe-up to the drop page
2 creator try-on clips featuring the sold-out-risk angle
1 podcast segment cut into a shoppable YouTube Short

whyItWorks: A host who lives in the culture makes a drop feel like a tip from a friend, not an ad, which converts far better than a banner. Mid-tier culture shows deliver the same tastemaker listeners as top-100 podcasts at a fraction of the CPM, and every read gets cut into a Reel and a Short so one recording earns twice across the feed and clip economy.

title: The $6,000 Shelf-to-Cart Haircare Play
objective: Build national awareness for a D2C haircare line and drive first purchases with a promo code baked into trusted host reads. Position the brand as an everyday staple, not a one-time novelty.

Reels showing the host using the product mid-routine with the promo code in caption and sticker
Featured blurb with code in the show's subscriber email

whyItWorks: When a host describes their own wash day on air, the product inherits the trust the audience already gives that voice. Sponsoring across multiple flights beats a single placement because repetition inside a familiar voice turns curiosity into a code redemption. title: The $5,500 Daily Fuel Energy Play
objective: Drive national trial for a new energy drink by tying it to the moments hosts already fuel up for: studio sessions, workouts, and late nights. Convert awareness into a sampling code.

mediaMix.detail: Live read at the top of the show framed around the host's own energy ritual, two flights on two shows
Fast-cut clips of hosts cracking a can before a set or a lift
Mid-roll product moment inside a video episode

whyItWorks: An energy drink lives or dies on association with high-energy moments, and a host naming when they reach for it is more persuasive than any tagline. Repeated sponsorship across flights builds the habit loop a single spot can't. Mid-tier culture shows over-index on the young, active listeners energy brands chase.
restrictionsNote: Some shows restrict energy/caffeine ads for younger-skewing audiences; confirm age-of-audience and any caffeine-claim disclaimers before booking.

title: The $4,500 Wind-Down Wellness Play
objective: Introduce a CBD wellness line as part of a nightly recovery routine and drive first orders via a trusted host-read discount code. Normalize the category through honest, personal storytelling.

mediaMix.detail: 60-second host read framed around post-grind recovery and better sleep, one flight per show
Subscriber blurb with code and a link to the starter bundle
Story sequence walking through the host's wind-down routine

whyItWorks: CBD is a trust-first category, and a host candidly describing how it fits their recovery removes the skepticism a banner ad triggers. Repetition inside that trusted voice normalizes the product over a few weeks in a way one placement never could. Mid-tier wellness-leaning culture shows reach curious buyers without premium-network waste.
restrictionsNote: CBD is a regulated category: no health or cure claims, adults 21+ targeting where required, and some shows and platforms prohibit CBD reads entirely, confirm eligibility before booking.

title: The $5,000 Know-Your-Rights Lead Play
objective: Generate qualified case inquiries for a personal injury and consumer firm through host reads that lead with a clear call to action and a dedicated intake number. Build trust before the click.

Repeating the intake CTA across flights keeps the number top of mind for the moment a listener actually needs it. budgetAllocation.label: Podcast host reads with promo codes

objective: Position a sports drink as the go-to fuel for workouts and game-day watching among an active, culturally engaged audience. Build broad awareness and everyday association.

mediaMix.detail: Mid-roll host reads tied to each host's workout or game-day routine
Reels of hosts refueling post-workout with the product in frame
Gym and game-day clips cut from show content for Shorts

whyItWorks: Sports and hip-hop audiences overlap heavily, so a multi-show buy across culture and sports podcasts assembles a national base of active listeners in one order. A host who name-checks the drink as part of their real routine makes it feel earned rather than sponsored, and the workout clips keep circulating on social long past the original read.

title: The $21,000 Consumer Tech Launch Play
objective: Launch a new consumer tech product by putting real units in hosts' hands and letting them demo it on air and on camera. Drive preorders and first-week sales.

mediaMix.detail: Host reads where each host demos one feature that fits how they actually use tech
Hands-on demo clips cut for Shorts and pushed from show channels
Unboxing and first-use Reels with a preorder link
Dedicated placements with a launch-window preorder code

whyItWorks: Tech buyers trust demonstrations over claims, and eight hosts showing the product in their own hands turns a launch into a wave of authentic hands-on proof across a national audience. The multi-show buy reaches early adopters inside the shows they already listen to, and each demo clip works twice as podcast content and as a preorder-driving social asset.

title: The $19,000 Premium Spirits Toast Play
objective: Build premium brand awareness for a spirit among an adult audience by owning celebration and cocktail moments across culture shows. Establish the brand as the pour worth reaching for.

mediaMix.detail: Mid-roll host reads on 21+ verified shows tied to celebration and cocktail moments
Age-gated Reels of hosts mixing a signature cocktail
Cocktail-build clips cut for show channels with age-restriction settings enabled

whyItWorks: Spirits marketing depends on aspiration and occasion, and a trusted host raising a glass makes the brand feel like the natural choice for a celebration. The multi-show buy assembles a national adult audience across culture shows in one order, and the signature-cocktail clips keep working socially as evergreen inspiration for the next toast.
restrictionsNote: 21+ only. All placements run on age-verified shows and channels with age-gating enabled on social and video; no targeting of under-21 audiences. Category exclusivity: no competing spirits or alcohol brands run concurrently on the same shows during the flight; include responsible-drinking messaging.

title: The $12,000 National Giving Drive Play
objective: Raise donations for a national nonprofit fundraiser by having hosts share why the cause matters and driving listeners to a tracked giving page. Optimize for donations per dollar spent.

mediaMix.detail: Heartfelt host reads where each host connects the cause to their own community
Dedicated placements with a matching-gift deadline and donate button
Story sets with the donation link sticker and a personal ask from the host

whyItWorks: People give when someone they trust asks them to, and a host tying a national cause to their own community turns an appeal into a personal invitation. The multi-show buy pools many loyal audiences into meaningful national reach on a modest budget, and each heartfelt read can be clipped so the ask keeps circulating through the giving window.

title: The $10,000 HBCU Recruiting Play
objective: Drive prospective-student inquiries and applications to a university by reaching college-bound listeners and their families through trusted culture shows. Optimize for info-request sign-ups.

mediaMix.detail: Host reads inviting listeners and their families to request info, hosts speaking to campus experience where relevant
Reels featuring campus life with a link to the inquiry form
Day-in-the-life campus clips with the apply link in caption

whyItWorks: College decisions are made across families, and culture podcasts reach both prospective students and the parents and mentors who guide them. A multi-show buy gathers a national pool of college-bound listeners in one order, and campus-life clips work twice as native podcast reads and as shareable social proof that keeps driving inquiries.

title: The $16,000 Wireless Switch Play
objective: Build awareness for a wireless carrier's switch offer and drive listeners to check eligibility. Balance broad reach with a clear promo-code call to action.

mediaMix.detail: Host reads on the switch offer with a per-show promo code, hosts framing it around real monthly savings
Reels of hosts explaining the plan with a link to check eligibility
Short offer-explainer clips cut for show channels
Live reads on the two largest shows to anchor the promo window

budgetAllocation.label: Podcast host reads with promo codes

whyItWorks: Switching carriers is a trust-and-value decision, and a host who frames the offer around real monthly savings makes the switch feel low-risk. The multi-show buy assembles a national audience of value-minded listeners, per-show promo codes turn that reach into clean attribution, and every offer-explainer clip keeps working on social through the promo window.
